ogTitle: Upper Macdonald vs Upper Main Arm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Upper Macdonald and Upper Main Arm.
heading: Upper Macdonald vs Upper Main Arm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Upper Macdonald, New South Wales and Upper Main Arm, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Upper Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Upper Main Arm (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Upper Macdonald or Upper Main Arm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Upper Macdonald has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Upper Main Arm scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Upper Macdonald scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Upper Main Arm scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Upper Macdonald or Upper Main Arm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Upper Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
